title: Runtime Lifecycle State Machine — Boot, Workspace, Session, Execution, Recovery
context: Tribunus has a coordination kernel (Valkey/PGlite), a campaign state machine (lane lifecycle: created→scouting→scoped→planning→...), session operations in ACP (new, load, resume, close), and runtime events (opencode.runtime_event.v1). What is missing is a documented, unified state machine for the application runtime itself: the outer lifecycle that governs boot, workspace management, session orchestration, crash recovery, and cockpit projection. Without this, subsystems cannot coordinate lifecycle transitions, crash recovery cannot identify what to preserve vs. rebuild, and the cockpit has no contract for what state to project.
decision:
Define the Runtime Lifecycle State Machine with the following states and transitions.

STATES:
- UNINITIALIZED: Process started, no state loaded.
- BOOTING: Dependency assembly, config loading, database migration, provider initialization, plugin loading.
- READY: All dependencies assembled, main window rendered, awaiting user action or deep link.
- WORKSPACE_OPENING: Workspace directory resolved, file watchers started, index hydration begun.
- WORKSPACE_READY: Workspace is open, repos attached, project context loaded.
- SESSION_ACTIVE: A session is running with an agent or user interaction in progress.
- SESSION_IDLE: Session exists but no active work; agent is parked.
- EXECUTING: Agent or tool execution in progress within a session.
- ARTIFACT_PRODUCING: Agent is producing artifacts (files, receipts, checkpoints).
- CHECKPOINTING: Session state is being serialized to durable storage (PGlite).
- SUSPENDED: Application state serialized and process may be stopped; resume restores.
- RESUMING: Restoring state from durable storage after suspend or crash.
- RECOVERING: Crash detected; reconciling durable state, rebuilding coordination state.
- CLOSING: Workspace and sessions being closed; resources released.
- TERMINATED: Clean shutdown complete.

VALID TRANSITIONS (from → to, event):
- UNINITIALIZED → BOOTING (boot.initiated)
- BOOTING → READY (boot.completed) or BOOTING → RECOVERING (crash.detected)
- READY → WORKSPACE_OPENING (workspace.open)
- WORKSPACE_OPENING → WORKSPACE_READY (workspace.ready) or WORKSPACE_OPENING → RECOVERING (crash.detected) or WORKSPACE_OPENING → CLOSING (workspace.invalid)
- WORKSPACE_READY → SESSION_ACTIVE (session.start) or WORKSPACE_READY → CLOSING (workspace.close)
- SESSION_ACTIVE → EXECUTING (agent.start) or SESSION_ACTIVE → SESSION_IDLE (agent.pause)
- EXECUTING → ARTIFACT_PRODUCING (artifact.produce) or EXECUTING → CHECKPOINTING (session.checkpoint) or EXECUTING → SESSION_ACTIVE (agent.complete)
- ARTIFACT_PRODUCING → CHECKPOINTING (checkpoint.requested) or ARTIFACT_PRODUCING → EXECUTING (artifact.complete)
- CHECKPOINTING → SESSION_ACTIVE (checkpoint.complete) or CHECKPOINTING → SUSPENDED (suspend.requested)
- SESSION_IDLE → SUSPENDED (suspend.requested) or SESSION_IDLE → EXECUTING (agent.resume) or SESSION_IDLE → CLOSING (session.close)
- SUSPENDED → RESUMING (resume.requested) or SUSPENDED → TERMINATED (process.exit)
- RESUMING → WORKSPACE_READY (resume.complete) or RESUMING → RECOVERING (resume.failed)
- Any state → RECOVERING (crash.detected) — crash recovery can be entered from any state
- RECOVERING → BOOTING (recovery.bootstrap) or RECOVERING → WORKSPACE_READY (recovery.complete)
- CLOSING → TERMINATED (close.complete) or CLOSING → RECOVERING (crash.detected)

CRASH RECOVERY SEMANTICS:
- PGlite state is durable and survives crashes. WorkItemTable, SessionTable, and recovery receipts are authoritative.
- Valkey state is coordination ephemera and is rebuilt from PGlite after crash.
- On crash entry: scan PGlite for non-terminal work items, abandoned sessions, and interrupted checkpoints.
- Rebuild protocol: reconcile PGlite facts against Valkey PEL state, acknowledge terminal entries, re-enqueue non-terminal entries, restore sorted-set schedules.
- Cockpit projection during recovery: show 'recovering' state with progress from RecoveryReceiptTable.
- Suspended state serializes: active session IDs, workspace path, open file handles (by path), UI position.
- Suspended state does NOT serialize: in-flight agent execution, tool execution, or Valkey stream state (those are rebuilt).

PROJECTION LIFECYCLE:
- The cockpit subscribes to runtime state changes via the runtime event bus.
- Every state transition emits a runtime event with the new state, timestamp, and transition metadata.
- The cockpit renders the current state as a status bar indicator and health dashboard.
- On state transitions that block user interaction (BOOTING, RECOVERING, CHECKPOINTING), the cockpit shows a progress overlay.
- On state transitions that produce artifacts (ARTIFACT_PRODUCING), the cockpit updates the artifact panel.

SUSPEND/RESUME CONTRACT:
- Suspend serializes to PGlite: active workspace identity, open session IDs with their last checkpoint, UI layout.
- Resume reads from PGlite: restores workspace, reopens sessions from last checkpoint, restores UI.
- In-flight agent execution is NOT suspended — it is checkpointed (agent state saved) and resumed (agent re-invoked from checkpoint).
- If the application crashes during suspend, the unsuspended state is recoverable from the last successful checkpoint.
- A resume after crash enters RECOVERING, not RESUMING — the distinction matters because crash recovery replays from PGlite, while normal resume replays from the suspend checkpoint.
consequences: This state machine becomes the outer governance contract for all subsystems. The coordination kernel, campaign state machine, ACP session operations, and cockpit projection all operate within specific runtime states and must respect the transition contract. Crash recovery now has explicit semantics: what is preserved (PGlite), what is rebuilt (Valkey), and what is lost (in-flight volatile state). The cockpit has a contract for what to render at each state. Suspend/resume is explicitly defined with serialization boundaries. This enables the unattended execution queue to know when it is safe to start, pause, resume, or recover work.
